Class RangedClass

java.lang.Object
logic.entity.Character
logic.entity.characterClass.RangedClass
All Implemented Interfaces:
Attackable, AttackAnimation, Damageable, UsesAmmo
Direct Known Subclasses:
Archer, Bubble, Mage
public abstract class RangedClass extends Character implements Attackable, UsesAmmo
Base class for ranged characters with ammo and reload mechanics.

Subclasses implement spawnRangedAttack(float, float, boolean, Player) to create projectile behavior while this class manages attack gating and ammo.

  • Constructor Details

    • RangedClass

      public RangedClass()
      Creates ranged class with default ranged stats and ammo setup.

    • RangedClass

      public RangedClass(int hp, int atk, int def, int attackRange, float attackSpeed, int maxAmmo)
      Creates ranged class with custom combat stats and ammo capacity.
      Parameters:
      hp - HP value
      atk - attack value
      def - defense value
      attackRange - range value
      attackSpeed - attack speed
      maxAmmo - max ammo capacity

  • Method Details

    • tryAttack

      public final void tryAttack(Player self, float startX, float startY, boolean facingRight)
      Template attack entry that enforces ammo/cooldown rules before spawning projectile.
      Parameters:
      self - owning player
      startX - spawn X
      startY - spawn Y
      facingRight - facing direction

    • spawnRangedAttack

      protected abstract void spawnRangedAttack(float startX, float startY, boolean facingRight, Player p)
      Subclass hook to spawn concrete ranged projectile attack.
      Parameters:
      startX - spawn X
      startY - spawn Y
      facingRight - facing direction
      p - owning player

      public boolean canShoot()
      Returns whether a shot can currently be fired.
      Specified by:
      canShoot in interface UsesAmmo
      Returns:
      true if not reloading, has ammo, and shoot delay elapsed

    • consumeAmmo

      public void consumeAmmo()
      Consumes one ammo and starts shoot cooldown. Triggers reload when empty.
      Specified by:
      consumeAmmo in interface UsesAmmo

      public void reload()
